PROFILE.DESCRIPTION: Java and Spring Boot developer focused on building scalable APIs, cloud applications, and clean architectures.

LIST.description: Working on projects for different clients, developing backend solutions with Java, Spring Boot and Postgres.

clients.name: Rede Compras
clients.description: Refactoring REST APIs and developing systems with TCP communication. Migrating applications from Java 17 to Java 21 and 25, participating in the virtual wallet project for LeCard cards, implementing observability in Java services, and creating a deployment pipeline with GitHub Actions.

clients.name: Top Doc
clients.description: I worked on migrating a legacy API from .NET 3 to .NET 9, leading refactoring efforts to improve architecture, separation of concerns, and adoption of best development practices.

LIST.description: Development of solutions for monitoring water consumption in reservoirs and condominiums. Creation of microservices in Python (Flask) for report generation using RabbitMQ and Redis, as well as the development of a web dashboard in React and TypeScript for data visualization. I also performed maintenance of queries in Oracle and developed bots for WhatsApp and Telegram.
Game developed autonomously in Unity with C#, in this project I worked as a programmer and team leader. The goal of the game is to raise awareness about not polluting the environment.

ABOUT.LABEL: About Me
ABOUT.TITLE: The person behind the code
ABOUT.SUBTITLE: Passionate about building scalable solutions that make a real difference. Here's a bit about who I am.
ABOUT.BIO_1: Hey! I'm Lucas Oliveira, a Back-End developer focused on Java and Spring Boot, with a growing passion for full-stack development. I love turning complex problems into clean, maintainable solutions.
ABOUT.BIO_2: I'm constantly exploring new technologies, contributing to open-source projects, and sharpening my skills in cloud architecture and distributed systems. When I'm not coding, I'm reading about software design patterns or experimenting with side projects.
